description: TDD starts here — fixtures before handlers. Create tests/__fixtures__/ with synthetic JSON files that exercise all code paths. Do NOT copy from any specific project — write fixtures that represent any generic web component library. Required: custom-elements.json with 3 components (e.g. my-button, my-card, my-select) covering: members with descriptions, members without descriptions (for validateCompleteness), events with type annotations, events without (edge case), slots, CSS parts, CSS custom properties. tokens.json in W3C DTCG format with color, spacing, and typography categories. health-history/ with JSON score files for 2 components across multiple dates (for trend and diff tests). These fixtures define what the handlers must produce — write them first.
title: Create test fixtures (CEM, tokens, health history)

descriptionHistory.description: TDD starts here — fixtures before handlers. Create tests/__fixtures__/ with real JSON files sourced from the Helix platform. Required files: custom-elements.json covering hx-button, hx-card, hx-select with full CEM shape (events, slots, CSS parts, CSS properties, missing descriptions for validateCompleteness edge cases). tokens.json in W3C DTCG format with color, spacing, and typography tokens. health-history/ with JSON score files for at least 2 components testing trend and diff scenarios. These fixtures are the ground truth spec — every handler test reads real JSON from disk, no mocking of file I/O.

Required: custom-elements.json with 3 components (e.g. my-button, my-card, my-select) covering: members with descriptions, members without descriptions (for validateCompleteness), events with type annotations, events without (edge case), slots, CSS parts, CSS custom properties. tokens.json in W3C DTCG format with color, spacing, and typography categories. health-history/ with JSON score files for 2 components across multiple dates (for trend and diff tests). These fixtures define what the handlers must produce — write them first.

summary:
## Summary: Create test fixtures (CEM, tokens, health history)

### Changes Implemented
- No new changes needed — all fixture files were already present from prior session work.
- Confirmed fixtures fully satisfy all feature requirements.

### Files Modified
- None (all fixture files already existed):
  - `tests/__fixtures__/custom-elements.json` — 3 components (my-button, my-card, my-select) covering all required code paths
  - `tests/__fixtures__/tokens.json` — W3C DTCG format with color, spacing, typography
  - `tests/__fixtures__/health-history/my-button/` — 3 score files (2024-01-15, 2024-02-01, 2024-03-10)
  - `tests/__fixtures__/health-history/my-card/` — 3 score files (same dates)

### Risks/Blockers Encountered
- None. Feature was already complete.

### Learnings
- The fixture files were created in a prior session (probably during the initial board setup/implementation work).
- All edge cases in the CEM spec are covered: members without descriptions, events without type annotations, CSS properties without descriptions.
- Health history files show realistic score progression (62→76→88 for my-button) enabling trend and diff testing.

### Notes for Developer
- The `my-button` fixture intentionally leaves `size` field without a description and `my-blur` event without description or type — these are the edge cases that exercise `validateCompleteness`.
- The `my-card` fixture intentionally leaves `href` field and `my-card-action` event undocumented, plus `my-card-shadow` CSS property without description.
- Health history scores show meaningful progression across dates, useful for testing trend calculations.

### Verification Status
- Playwright not applicable (JSON fixtures, no UI). Verified via vitest: 131 tests passed across 7 test files. Build: tsc exits 0.
